verdicts.reason: URL regex silently accepts malformed URLs; param parsing fails on edge cases (empty keys, unencoded ampersands); no validation of rebuild inputs; clipboard fallback swallows errors; race condition in bulk deduplication.

CSP policy allows 'unsafe-inline' and connect-src *, enabling XSS and data exfiltration; password field displayed in plaintext in rebuild widget; URL regex extraction vulnerable to ReDoS; no input validation on rebuild fields before URL construction.

Injected accessibility override uses !important to force 19px base font and 15px minimum on all text, breaking the project's intentional compact monospace design and creating severe visual hierarchy collapse.
No README.md exists; tool has three major features (Dissect, Resolve, Bulk) with zero discoverable documentation of what each does, how to use them, or what the output means.

Real recurring use case: developers and ops need to inspect, decode, and reconstruct URLs during debugging, API integration, and log analysis. This tool solves three distinct workflows (dissect, resolve, bulk extract) that are not trivially available in browser dev tools.
Signature move: live-rebuild widget that lets you edit URL components and watch the reconstructed URL update in real-time—no other URL parser does this interactively.

Deliverable violates documented KEEP lesson: 'String.prototype.split(x).join(y) replaces .replace(/x/g, y) when x is "' — code uses .replace(/g) regex patterns with embedded quotes, triggering the known test_project.py brace-balance bug.
